file_name: GWG HOLDINGS, INC. - ORDERLY MARKETING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall execute and deliver all such further instruments and documents and take all such other actions as may reasonably be required to carry out the transactions contemplated hereby and to evidence the fulfillment of the agreements herein contained.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall attempt to execute and deliver further instruments and documents and take actions as they individually deem necessary to fulfill the agreements herein contained.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the parties to execute and deliver documents and take actions to fulfill the agreement. The modified text introduces ambiguity by stating that the parties only 'attempt' to do so and can take actions as they 'individually deem necessary,' potentially contradicting the original intent of mutual cooperation and creating uncertainty in enforcing the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law - Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: New York law implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract. By changing 'shall execute and deliver' to 'shall attempt,' and introducing individual discretion, the modified text undermines this duty of good faith by allowing parties to potentially avoid fulfilling their contractual obligations based on their individual assessment, creating a legal contradiction.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: NYS Open Legislation | NYSenate.gov Sorry, you need to enable JavaScript to visit this website. Skip to main content Legislation Search OpenLegislation Statutes Search Term Search icon Search The Laws of New York Consolidated Laws of New York CHAPTER 38 Uniform Commercial Code ARTICLE 1 No title PART 3 Territorial Applicability and General Rules previous SECTION 1-303 Course of Performance, Course of Dealing, and Usage of Trade up PART 3 Territorial Applicability and General Rules next SECTION 1-305 Remedies to be Liberally Administered This entry was published on 2014-12-26 The selection dates indicate all change milestones for the entire volume, not just the location being viewed. Specifying a milestone date will retrieve the most recent version of the location before that date. See most recent version before or on: 2014-09-22 2014-12-26 2015-01-02 2015-02-20 2015-02-27 2016-05-20 2016-08-26 2016-09-16 2018-08-31 2018-10-05 2021-12-03 2022-01-28 2022-06-03 Share Facebook Email SECTION 1-304 Obligation of Good Faith Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) CHAPTER 38, ARTICLE 1, PART 3 Section 1--304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within this act imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation of the parties to fulfill the agreement, potentially undermining the covenant of good faith and fair dealing implied in every contract as stated in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing UCC 1-304, explicitly states, 'Every contract or duty within this act imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement.' By changing 'shall execute and deliver' to 'shall attempt' and introducing individual discretion, the modified text allows parties to avoid fulfilling contractual obligations based on their individual assessment. This directly contradicts the requirement for good faith in performance and enforcement because attempting is not the same as doing, and individual assessment may not align with the original intent of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This OMA, and all claims or causes of action (whether in contract or tort) that may be based upon, arise out of or relate to this OMA or the negotiation, execution or performance of this OMA (including any claim or cause of action based upon, arising out of or related to any representation or warranty made in or in connection with this OMA), will be construed in accordance with and governed by the law of the State of New York without regard to principles of conflicts of laws that would result in the application of the law of any other jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: This OMA, and all claims or causes of action related to it, will be governed by the laws most favorable to GWG Holdings, Inc., as determined by GWG Holdings, Inc. in its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that New York law governs the agreement, which is a clear and standard choice of law provision. The modified text states that the laws most favorable to GWG Holdings, Inc., as determined by GWG Holdings, Inc., will govern, which could potentially conflict with New York law and create uncertainty as to which jurisdiction's laws apply.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law - Choice of Law Principles
perturbation.law_explanation: The Restatement of Conflict of Laws outlines principles for determining which law governs a contract. While parties can generally choose the governing law, the choice must be reasonable and not violate public policy. Granting one party the unilateral right to choose the most favorable law at their discretion is likely unenforceable and contradicts established choice of law principles.

perturbation.justification: The original contract specifies New York law, providing certainty. The changed text allows GWG Holdings, Inc. to select the governing law, potentially overriding New York law. The LLM explanation states this contradicts choice of law principles. The scraped snippets are not helpful, but the change creates a clear conflict by allowing a party to unilaterally select laws most favorable to itself, which undermines established legal frameworks for governing contracts.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the Parties hereto represents, several and not jointly, that it has taken all action required of it to duly authorize this OMA and that no further action or approval is required on its behalf and, when executed and delivered, this OMA constitutes a valid and binding obligation of such Party, enforceable in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Parties hereto represents, based on their current understanding, that they have taken some action to authorize this OMA, and it may or may not constitute a valid and binding obligation.
perturbation.explanation: The original text offers a clear representation that each party has taken all necessary actions to authorize the agreement, making it a valid and binding obligation. The modified text weakens this representation by stating it's based on 'current understanding' and casting doubt on whether the OMA constitutes a valid and binding obligation, creating significant uncertainty and potentially rendering the contract unenforceable.
perturbation.law_explanation: New York General Obligations Law requires certain agreements to be in writing and signed to be enforceable. By suggesting that the agreement may not be valid or binding, the modified text directly undermines the enforceability requirements stipulated by the law. The representation of enforceability is essential for a contract, and undermining it creates a significant legal issue.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: NYS Open Legislation | NYSenate.gov Sorry, you need to enable JavaScript to visit this website. Skip to main content Legislation Search OpenLegislation Statutes Search Term Search icon Search The Laws of New York Consolidated Laws of New York CHAPTER 24-A General Obligations ARTICLE 5 Creation, Definition and Enforcement of Contractual Obligations TITLE 7 Requirements of Writing, Execution or Acknowledgment For Effectiveness or Enforceability up TITLE 7 Requirements of Writing, Execution or Acknowledgment For Effectiveness or Enforceability next SECTION 5-702 Requirements for use of plain language in consumer transactions This entry was published on 2014-09-22 The selection dates indicate all change milestones for the entire volume, not just the location being viewed. Specifying a milestone date will retrieve the most recent version of the location before that date. See most recent version before or on: 2014-09-22 2015-10-09 2017-10-27 2018-04-20 2018-07-06 2018-07-13 2018-08-31 2019-01-11 2019-01-18 2019-06-28 2019-07-05 2019-07-19 2019-08-16 2019-10-18 2019-11-22 2019-12-13 2020-02-21 2020-04-10 2020-12-18 2021-01-08 2021-04-02 2021-04-09 2021-06-18 2021-09-24 2021-12-31 2022-02-25 2022-03-04 2022-12-30 2023-01-06 2023-10-27 2023-11-26 2024-12-20 2025-01-03 Share Facebook Email SECTION 5-701 Agreements required to be in writing General Obligations (GOB) CHAPTER 24-A, ARTICLE 5, TITLE 7 § 5-701. Agreements required to be in writing. a. Every agreement, promise or undertaking is void, unless it or some note or memorandum thereof be in writing, and subscribed by the party to be charged therewith, or by his lawful agent, if such agreement, promise or undertaking: 1. By its terms is not to be performed within one year from the making thereof or the performance of which is not to be completed before the end of a lifetime; 2. Is a special promise to answer for the debt, default or miscarriage of another person; 3. Is made in consideration of marriage, except mutual promises to marry; 5. Is a subsequent or new promise to pay a debt discharged in bankruptcy; 6. Notwithstanding section 2-201 of the uniform commercial code, if the goods be sold at public auction, and the auctioneer at the time of the sale, enters in a sale book, a memorandum specifying the nature and price of the property sold, the terms of the sale, the name of the purchaser, and the name of the person on whose account the sale was made, such memorandum is equivalent in effect to a note of the contract or sale, subscribed by the party to be charged therewith; 9. Is a contract to assign or an assignment, with or without consideration to the promisor, of a life or health or accident insurance policy, or a promise, with or without consideration to the promisor, to name a beneficiary of any such policy. This provision shall not apply to a policy of industrial life or health or accident insurance. 10. Is a contract to pay compensation for services rendered in negotiating a loan, or in negotiating the purchase, sale, exchange, renting or leasing of any real estate or interest therein, or of a business opportunity, business, its good will, inventory, fixtures or an interest therein, including a majority of the voting stock interest in a corporation and including the creating of a partnership interest.
perturbation.justification: The modified text undermines the enforceability of the contract by suggesting it 'may or may not constitute a valid and binding obligation.' This contradicts the principle that agreements should be binding, as supported by NY General Obligations Law, which requires certain agreements to be in writing to be enforceable (NYS Open Legislation | NYSenate.gov, SECTION 5-701). By casting doubt on the validity and binding nature of the agreement, the changed text directly opposes the law's aim to ensure enforceable written agreements.
